overview.short: Kwangtang Island Wildlife Sanctuary (also spelled Kwangtung Island Wildlife Sanctuary) is a protected area located on North Andaman Island in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, India
overview.description: Kwangtang Island Wildlife Sanctuary (also spelled Kwangtung Island Wildlife Sanctuary) is a protected area located on North Andaman Island in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, India. It spans approximately 0.57 km² and was officially notified in 1987 as part of the Union Territory’s network of wildlife sanctuaries under IUCN Category IV. It lies southeast of the McPherson Bay.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kwangtung Island Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kwangtung Island Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kwangtung Island Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Veer Savarkar International Airport, Port Blair (199 km) and the nearest railway station is Puri Railway Station (1046 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kwangtung Island Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kwangtung Island Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kwangtung Island Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Kwangtung Island Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kwangtung Island Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Smith Island (Andaman Islands).
